message:
refactor(indexer+lib+asset_state): route every `meta.json`-based existence/freshness check through the new searchable_index_* helpers so federated (multi-shard) installs work

Sweep across the codebase replacing the legacy
`index_path.join("meta.json").exists()` pattern with the new
abstraction added in commit bd493536. A singleton-generation install
has a `meta.json` directly under the index dir, but a federated
install has its shards alongside `federated-search-manifest.json`
instead — and every previous check was asking "is there a meta.json
right here?" which would have returned false for federated installs
even when they contained a fully-built searchable index.

src/search/tantivy.rs already provides the three-function contract:
- `searchable_index_exists(&Path) -> bool`
- `searchable_index_modified_time(&Path) -> Option<SystemTime>`
- `searchable_index_fingerprint(&Path) -> Result<Option<String>>`
- `searchable_index_summary(&Path) -> Result<Option<SearchableIndexSummary>>`

This commit makes the rest of the code actually use them.

src/lib.rs
- `cass_lexical_index_initialized(data_dir)` — was
  `expected_index_dir(data_dir).join("meta.json").exists()`. Now
  `searchable_index_exists(&expected_index_dir(data_dir))`.
- `state_meta_json(...)`:
  * The `lexical_index_initialized` boolean reads through
    `searchable_index_exists(&index_path)` now, same reason.
  * The `last_indexed_at` fallback used to mtime-stat
    `meta.json`; now delegates to
    `searchable_index_modified_time(&index_path)` which returns
    the latest mtime across shard segment files, so federated
    installs report a sensible "last activity" timestamp.
- `run_cli_search(...)`: the `tantivy_index_initialized` branch
  switches to `searchable_index_exists(&index_path)` so
  `cass search` correctly detects a populated federated install.

src/indexer/mod.rs
- `index_meta_fingerprint(index_path)` — was reading `meta.json`
  bytes directly and hashing with blake3. Delegates entirely to
  `searchable_index_fingerprint(index_path)`, which already
  computes the combined fingerprint across all shards (using
  schema_hash + per-shard meta_fingerprint), so the archive-
  fingerprint-keyed skip logic added in commit 82d8d70e continues
  to work byte-identically on singleton installs and now also
  correctly invalidates when any shard changes on federated
  installs.
- `live_tantivy_doc_count(index_path)` — was calling
  `frankensearch::lexical::cass_open_search_reader` directly and
  reading `reader.searcher().num_docs()`. Now uses
  `searchable_index_summary(index_path)` which already knows how
  to aggregate docs across all shards on federated installs. Error
  branch still logs the same `debug!` with the same
  "probing live tantivy reader failed" message.
- `run_index(...)`: the `tantivy_requires_rebuild` decision used
  to check `meta.json` existence. Switches to
  `searchable_index_exists(&index_path)`, so a federated install
  is not treated as "needs rebuild" just because there's no
  top-level meta.json.

src/search/asset_state.rs
- `lexical_state_from_observations`: the local `exists` boolean
  switches from `index_path.join("meta.json").exists()` to
  `searchable_index_exists(index_path)`. Same reasoning as the
  lib.rs/indexer call sites — the lexical-state state machine
  must treat federated installs as "exists" when detecting
  existing-index resume scenarios.

Zero behavioural change on singleton installs (the new helpers
fall back to the singleton `meta.json` path under the hood).
Federated installs stop getting misdiagnosed as "index missing"
at a handful of places and start reporting correct mtime / doc
count / fingerprint / existence.
